recent_posts.text:
Bitcoin ETF flows reverse course with $181M net inflow on July 14, ending 8-week outflow streak –
Link
Bitcoin spot ETFs saw $181 million in net inflows on July 14, led by BlackRock's IBIT at $139 million. Ethereum ETFs also posted $58.3 million in net inflows, with all ten ETFs in positive territory.
Matters because:
The flow reversal suggests institutional buyers may be returning after weeks of selling pressure.

CXMT (ChangXin Memory Technologies) listed on Hyperliquid pre-IPO perps
• China's SK Hynix, China’s largest and the world’s fourth-largest DRAM manufacture (~ 7-9% global DRAM market share)
• Targeted for a July 27th IPO on the Shanghai Stock Exchange STAR Market
• One of Asia’s largest IPOs of 2026 and China’s biggest semiconductor listing in years
• The company received heavy state backing in the form of capital, land, policy support, and alignment with national semiconductor self-reliance goals (part of broader “Made in China 2025” and dual-circulation strategies)
• IPO proceeds are earmarked for further fab expansion, capacity ramps through 2026-2028, and technology upgrades aimed at AI server applications.
SOURCE

Nobody is close to breaking ECC
Although theoretical advances have dramatically reduced the quantum hardware needed to break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, current quantum computers are still orders of magnitude away from having enough error-corrected qubits to execute the attack
Consequently, standard internet protocols and cryptocurrencies face no immediate risk, though exposed public addresses will eventually need to transition to quantum-resistant encryption
https://derrickcui.substack.com/p/nobody-is-close-to-breaking-ecc

Latent Space as a New Medium
AI’s massive compression of human knowledge creates high-dimensional "latent spaces" that map concepts as directional vectors
These latent spaces will serve as a new creative medium, enabling scientists and artists to prospect gaps between ideas, simulate realities, and uncover hidden meta-patterns
https://kevinkelly.substack.com/p/latent-space-as-a-new-medium

Daily news snapshot: Altcoins watchlist
1. Aave (AAVE): +5.8% (24h)
• Market cap: $12.4 billion
• Aave is benefiting from the UK's new capital gains tax deferral for DeFi lending positions and its adoption of Chainlink CCIP as default cross-chain infrastructure.
Matters because:
The mix of policy support and infrastructure upgrades is a constructive signal for DeFi lending adoption.
2. Chainlink (LINK): +4.2% (24h)
• Market cap: $18.7 billion
• Chainlink won major institutional integrations, including U.S. Department of Commerce data feeds, Central Bank of Brazil-HKMA settlement pilots, and Mantle's $2.5B CCIP migration.
• Active network wallets hit an all-time high of 900,000.
Matters because:
Institutional usage and wallet growth suggest accumulation is happening even while price remains suppressed.
3. Solana (SOL): +3.1% (24h)
• Market cap: $89.3 billion
• Solana apps generated record $17M in weekly revenue and set a new $4.15B 24h DEX volume record.
• SBI Holdings launched a Japan-focused tokenization partnership, SBI Solana Global.
Matters because:
The revenue and volume records reinforce Solana's lead in onchain activity and institutional tokenization narratives.
https://levels.io/the-everything-chart

tweet.text:
Wix at 1x ARR is not a SaaS bloodbath. 

It is a reckoning for businesses that were always just sales and marketing operations dressed up as software.

1. The client-server to SaaS shift was more disruptive than anything AI is doing to software right now. This is incremental. The market is finally pricing it that way.

2. SaaS was never that hard to spin up. When the moat is a sales team and a logo, the multiple compresses. That is not a surprise, that is gravity.

3. The companies that survive this are the ones with complex customer relationships and data sets that cannot be recreated. Wix does not have that. Base44 might not either.

4. Vibe coding tools doing $170M ARR getting priced at $2.1BN is the same mistake in reverse. Getting real workflow change adopted at scale takes longer than the ARR curve suggests.

5. The best software companies right now are not pure software companies. The ones with physical world integration and proprietary data loops are harder to replicate and har

Galaxy Research: Inference Capital Markets
> A new onchain financial layer is emerging around AI inference, using crypto to tokenize compute access, finance GPU infrastructure, and create markets for AI services.
> For now, inference capital markets, both onchain and off, are tiny relative to the growth of the AI industry. For onchain offerings to scale, they have to prove the advantage they bring is sustainable.
Source:
https://www.galaxy.com/insights/research/inference-capital-markets-ai-compute-gpu-futures-onchain-crypto

Fed rate hike expectations surge on inflation concerns –
Link
Fed Governor Christopher Waller said the FOMC may need to raise rates in the near term if core inflation stays elevated. Markets now price about a 50% chance of a July hike, up from ≈10% days ago. Bitcoin is down 2%+ to around $62,380, while U.S. two-year Treasury yields rose to 4.29%.
Matters because:
Higher-for-longer rates and renewed hike odds add pressure to BTC and the broader risk backdrop.

TradFi Doesn’t Want DeFi. It Wants Blockchain.
> Institutions aren’t somehow merging with DeFi.
> Instead, they’re selectively using the parts of DeFi that fit within their operating constraints and discarding the parts that do not; reconfiguring DeFi around institutional requirements.
> We are beginning to see emergence of a new category built on blockchain rails but optimized for institutional constraints: programmable financial infrastructure.
Source:
https://a16zcrypto.com/posts/article/tradfi-doesnt-want-defi-it-wants-blockchain

recent_posts.text:
Aave adopts Chainlink CCIP for its cross-chain mobile app –
Link
Aave integrated Chainlink's Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) to power its new mobile app. CCIP scored highest on cross-chain security in Llama Risk's updated Aave Risk Framework and is said to introduce no new trust assumptions.
Matters because:
CCIP gives Aave a cross-chain path with a security-first framing, which matters for a major lending protocol.

tweet.text:
NEW: DeFi’s largest lending protocol @aave adopts Chainlink CCIP as the cross-chain infra powering its new mobile app.

This follows @LlamaRisk's updated Aave Risk Framework, where CCIP scored the highest on cross-chain security, introducing no new trust assumptions.
